item.title: How Did ‘Gatekeeping’ Become a Cardinal Sin?
item.description: We decided that filtering information was malicious. That didn’t mean we stopped doing it.

item.title: The Next Phase of the Immigration Crackdown Is Quieter — and More Destabilizing
item.description: Self-deportation is an idea with deep roots. It’s making a comeback.

item.title: Forks in the Dishwasher — Tines Up or Down?
item.description: A ruling on a timeless marital dispute.

item.title: ‘It Was Survival Mode’: The Lunar Mission That Nearly Ended in Disaster
item.description: Fifty-six years ago, after a tense race to save the Apollo 13 crew, the astronauts finally splashed down safely. Here’s what flight directors who got them home remember.

item.title: Lena Dunham Is Still Trying to Figure Out Why People Hated Her So Much
item.description: The writer, actor and lightning rod is not done sharing yet.

item.title: Is It OK to Lie in Order to Feed Hungry Families?
item.description: I worry that taking extra food for the people our organization serves means that others won’t get what they need.

item.title: The Rise and Fall and Rise of Michael Jackson
item.description: A new biopic is the latest move in the Jackson estate’s posthumous — and lucrative — rehabilitation campaign.

item.title: I Feel So Sorry for My A.I. Sunglasses
item.description: Plenty of people hate Mark Zuckerberg’s superintelligent, supercharged spectacles. I was ready to hate them, too.

item.title: My Longtime Housekeeper’s Work Is Slipping. What Should I Do?
item.description: We know she depends on this income, and we have hesitated to raise the issue with her.

item.title: Should You Procrastinate Productively — or by Having Fun?
item.description: A ruling on a dispute over the proper method of avoidance.

item.title: It’s an Obscure Psychedelic Used to Treat Trauma. Could It Help Me?
item.description: Veterans and others who have suffered trauma and injuries are flocking to clinics around the world to take ibogaine. My own reason was deeply personal.

item.title: My Husband’s Illness Has Me Feeling Trapped. Do I Have to Stay?
item.description: I don’t feel morally that I could leave. But I have healthy years ahead and I wish to enjoy what’s left of my life.

item.title: Violence Shaped Charlize Theron. It Doesn’t Define Her.
item.description: The Oscar-winning actress on pain, healing and becoming an action hero.

item.title: The Generations Fantasizing About Boring Office Jobs
item.description: “Day in my life” videos filmed at corporate offices fetishize white-collar minutiae. But they reveal something deeper.
